ocr.text: In the increasingly competitive landscape of healthcare services across the Asia-Pacific region, the importance of patient
satisfaction cannot be overstated. Institutions are continually striving to enhance their service delivery models, catering to the
evolving expectations of patients. This report presents a comprehensive analysis of patient satisfaction scores, with a particular
focus on metrics such as Inpatient Care and Outpatient Care. In 2023, HCA Healthcare achieved an Inpatient Care satisfaction
score of 90.5, reflecting a slight increase from the previous year, demonstrating the organization's commitment to improving the
patient experience within hospital settings. Conversely, outpatient satisfaction scores, while still commendable, showed a

modest score of 88.1, indicating areas that require further enhancement to meet patient expectations. 

Additionally, the report
highlights the significance of telehealth services, which have gained traction in recent years, particularly in the wake of the
COVID-19 pandemic.
PatientSatisfaction  Score
Year  TotalPatients  GrowthRate(%)
OverallSatisfaction  AverageScore  Change(%)  TopServices
ServiceType  Satisfaction(%)  CAGR(%)  MarketShare(%)  EmergencyCare  85.2
InpatientCare  90.5  2.3  26.7  OutpatientCare  88.1  1.9  15.3  Telehealth
Telehealth's total satisfaction score demonstrates its growing acceptance among patients as a viable alternative to traditional in-
person consultations. This emerging service line not only expands access to care but also enables healthcare providers to
connect with patients more efficiently. However, it is crucial to note that while the satisfaction scores for Inpatient Care and
Outpatient Care are robust, the reported score of 15.3 for telehealth reflects a need for continuous improvement, particularly in

areas such as technology integration and user experience. 

In summary, the evolving patient satisfaction metrics, including an
Inpatient Care score of 90.5 and an Outpatient Care score of 88.1, underscore the effectiveness of HCA Healthcare's strategies
in enhancing patient experience. As the organization navigates the complexities of patient care in the Asia-Pacific region, these
insights will be instrumental in guiding future initiatives aimed at improving overall satisfaction levels. The commitment to
patient-centered care, alongside ongoing evaluations of service delivery, remains paramount as healthcare providers adapt to the
dynamic needs of their patient populations.
